prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a statistical model (Black-Scholes-Merton style option pricing framework for stock price simulation) that assumes future price movements follow a log-normal distribution. The point estimate is set to the current price ($77.74), assuming zero drift over a short 18-day period for a neutral forecast. The confidence intervals are calculated using an assumed historical daily volatility for CSCO of 1.2% (leading to an annualized volatility of approximately 19.05%). This daily volatility is scaled by the square root of 18 days to project the expected standard deviation of returns over the prediction horizon. The bounds of the confidence intervals are then derived from standard normal distribution z-scores corresponding to the desired confidence levels (0.6745 for 50%, 1.2816 for 80%, and 1.6449 for 90%). It is crucial to understand that this is a statistical projection based on historical patterns and assumptions, and actual future prices can deviate significantly.
